Milwaukee crime family.description: The Milwaukee crime family was an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, founded around 1918 by Vito Guardalabene. At its peak, it had over 50 made members and maintained links to other Midwestern Mafia families. The family was heavily involved in skimming revenue from Las Vegas casinos under boss Frank Balistrieri. After Balistrieri's death in 1993, the Chicago Outfit absorbed the family, and it ceased to exist as an independent organization by 2024.

descriptions: Bonanno family is allied with Milwaukee crime family per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Milwaukee crime family

descriptions: The Outfit expanded into Milwaukee as the Balistrieri family declined, implying a takeover or displacement.
edges.evidence_quote: Monteleone was also responsible for expanding the Outfit's operations into Milwaukee as the Balistrieri family declined during the 1990s.

descriptions: The Milwaukee crime family is listed as an ally of the Chicago Outfit in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Milwaukee crime family

descriptions: Milwaukee family was represented by the Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Milwaukee crime family

descriptions: Cleveland, Kansas City, and Milwaukee families cooperated to influence the Teamsters pension fund.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cleveland, Kansas City and Milwaukee crime families also exerted influence over the multibillion-dollar Central States Pension Fund of the International Brotherhood of Teamsters (IBT)

descriptions: The Kansas City crime family is allied with the Milwaukee crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chicago Outfit , Cleveland crime family , Colorado crime family , Los Angeles crime family , Milwaukee crime family , St. Louis crime family

descriptions: The Bonanno crime family is listed as an ally of the Milwaukee family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family

descriptions: The Milwaukee family maintained links with the Chicago Outfit.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization maintained links to other Midwestern Mafia families, including those in Chicago and Kansas City

descriptions: The Chicago Outfit absorbed the Milwaukee family after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: After the death of Balistrieri in 1993, the Chicago Outfit began gaining control over illegal rackets in the Milwaukee area. The Milwaukee family ultimately ceased to exist as an independent organization and was largely absorbed by the Chicago family.

descriptions: The Cleveland crime family is listed as an ally of the Milwaukee family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cleveland crime family

descriptions: The Milwaukee family maintained links with the Kansas City crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization maintained links to other Midwestern Mafia families, including those in Chicago and Kansas City

descriptions: A member of the Madison crime family transferred to the Milwaukee family after the former disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: John Candela — former member of the Madison crime family ; transferred to the Milwaukee family after the family disbanded.

descriptions: The Rockford crime family is listed as an ally of the Milwaukee family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rockford crime family

descriptions: The St. Louis crime family is listed as an ally of the Milwaukee family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: St. Louis crime family

descriptions: Rockford boss Zammuto had close ties with Milwaukee boss Balistrieri.
edges.evidence_quote: As boss Zammuto maintained close ties and friendships with ... Milwaukee family boss Frank Balistrieri.

descriptions: Rockford underboss Vince worked with Milwaukee boss Balistrieri in illegal rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, the FBI revealed that Vince had become Rockford's underboss and had been working with Milwaukee family boss Frank Balistrieri in a number of illegal rackets.

descriptions: Rockford boss Gulotta had close friendships with Milwaukee boss Balistrieri.
edges.evidence_quote: Gulotta maintained close friendships with Zammuto and Milwaukee boss Frank Balistrieri.

descriptions: Infobox lists Milwaukee crime family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family , Chicago Outfit , Milwaukee crime family , Springfield crime family

Fasel Gang.description: A group of Swiss criminals active from 1977 to 1991, composed of Jacques Fasel, Daniel Bloch, and a third accomplice. They claimed to fight for anarchism and social justice, committing a series of robberies in Switzerland. The group was known for its escapes from prison and was sentenced in 1985.

descriptions: Los Gallegos son el brazo armado del Tren de Aragua en Chile.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Gallegos, 'brazo armado' del Tren de Aragua en Chile

descriptions: Los Gallegos es una célula del Tren de Aragua en España, liderada por un miembro del Tren de Aragua.
edges.evidence_quote: Luis José Rodríguez R. alias "Mamera" , del conocido grupo criminal, Los Gallegos, residía en España desde 2023, era solicitado por el gobierno del Perú por Interpol , está acusado de matar, en enero de 2020, a un hombre en el interior de un local de comida rápida de Lima y vinculado con la muerte de dos jóvenes venezolanas. Es el máximo líder en España

descriptions: The Pranno were part of the Perna faction.
edges.evidence_quote: da un lato i Perna-Pranno-Vitelli

descriptions: East Coast Riders members patched over to Bacchus.
edges.evidence_quote: On 10 January 2010, six members of the East Coast Riders pulled up to the Bacchus clubhouse in Hillsborough, New Brunswick to perform a ceremony where they burned their old biker jackets and put on new biker jackets with the Bacchus patch.

descriptions: Las Cachorras are a group of female hitmen integrated within Los Zetas.
edges.evidence_quote: un grupo de sicarias integradas por el Cártel de Los Zetas

Banda della Magliana.description: The Banda della Magliana was a mafia-type criminal organization based in Rome, Italy, active from the late 1970s until 1999. It unified the fragmented local underworld under a single structure, engaging in kidnappings, gambling, drug trafficking, and robberies. The group had connections with other Italian criminal organizations, Masonic lodges, far-right terrorists, and intelligence services. It was involved in several high-profile Italian historical events, including the Moro case and the disappearance of Emanuela Orlandi.

descriptions: The text states 'Ndrangheta has relationships with all Italian mafias, including Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto e ha rapporti con tutte le mafie italiane : Cosa nostra , Camorra , Banda della Magliana , Sacra Corona Unita , Basilischi .

descriptions: De Stefano member Giorgio had contacts with Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: From his law office in Rome, he also had contacts with the Banda della Magliana.

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone had contacts with the Banda della Magliana through its treasurer.
edges.evidence_quote: È noto che esponenti dei Facchineri-Varone a Roma hanno avuto contatti con Enrico Nicoletti , tesoriere della Banda della Magliana .

descriptions: Sebastiano Romeo was involved with Banda della Magliana in the search for Aldo Moro.
edges.evidence_quote: Sebastiano Romeo , secondo Francesco Fonti viene coinvolto insieme alla Banda della Magliana nella ricerca di Aldo Moro .

descriptions: The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian government claimed that the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with and tied to other criminal organizations, such as Cosa Nostra, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana was tied to 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was tied to other Italian criminal organizations such as the Cosa Nostra, Camorra and the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: Marcello Colafigli, a Banda della Magliana member, maintained contacts with 'Ndrangheta for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenendo rapporti con esponenti della 'ndrangheta , della camorra , della mafia foggiana e con albanesi inseriti in un cartello narcos sudamericano

descriptions: Banda della Magliana had close ties with the three southern mafias, including 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: la cosiddetta Banda della Magliana, che ebbe stretti legami con le tre mafie meridionali

descriptions: The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian government claimed that the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with and tied to other criminal organizations, such as Cosa Nostra, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The infobox lists Camorra as an ally of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NAR, Sicilian Mafia, Camorra

descriptions: Banda della Magliana was tied to Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was tied to other Italian criminal organizations such as the Cosa Nostra, Camorra and the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana established contacts with the Camorra.

descriptions: Claudio Sicilia revealed contacts between Banda della Magliana and the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: Sicilia è anche il primo a parlare dei contatti della Banda con la camorra di Lorenzo Nuvoletta e Michele Zaza

descriptions: Marcello Colafigli, a Banda della Magliana member, maintained contacts with Camorra for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenendo rapporti con esponenti della 'ndrangheta , della camorra , della mafia foggiana e con albanesi inseriti in un cartello narcos sudamericano

edges.evidence_quote: la cosiddetta Banda della Magliana, che ebbe stretti legami con le tre mafie meridionali

descriptions: Clan Carminati had its roots in Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 dicembre 2014 l'ex NAR Massimo Carminati, vicino alla Banda già dalla fine degli anni ‘70, viene arrestato assieme ad alcuni complici nell'ambito dell'operazione Mafia Capitale ; chiamata come Cupola Romana o ancora Clan Carminati , questa organizzazione operava a Roma già a partire dal 2000 circa, affondando le sue radici nelle rapine dei NAR degli anni ottanta e successivamente nella stessa banda della Magliana.

descriptions: Clan Spada contacted Franco Colò, a former Banda della Magliana member, to expand their videopoker business.
edges.evidence_quote: il gruppo degli "zingari" per allargare i propri traffici attraverso i videopoker si fossero messi in contatto con Franco Colò detto "il ciccione", storico braccio destro di Renatino De Pedis ai tempi della Banda

descriptions: Riccardo Sibio of the Fasciani clan often worked for Banda della Magliana members.
edges.evidence_quote: Riccardo Sibio, appartenente al clan dei Fasciani e spesso al servizio anche dei componenti della Banda

descriptions: The Clan dei Marsigliesi preceded the Banda della Magliana and changed the criminal landscape in Rome, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: con l'avvento del clan dei marsigliesi di Albert Bergamelli , Maffeo Bellicini e Jacques Berenguer trasferitisi nella capitale per dare vita a un redditizio business dello spaccio di eroina e, soprattutto, dei sequestri di persona, si determinò un deciso cambiamento dei rapporti di forze all'interno della piccola e frammentata malavita capitolina

descriptions: Banda della Magliana agreed to finance Costruiamo l'azione in exchange for favorable psychiatric evaluations.
edges.evidence_quote: da quegli incontri uscì un accordo di massima tra il professore e la banda che prevedeva finanziamenti per il gruppo neofascista in cambio di perizie medico psichiatriche compiacenti

descriptions: The infobox lists NAR as an ally of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NAR, Sicilian Mafia, Camorra

descriptions: Banda della Magliana commissioned murders from NAR members.
edges.evidence_quote: la Banda, di tanto in tanto commissionava ai giovani fascisti anche di eliminare alcune persone poco gradite

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana and NAR had converging interests, exemplified by a joint attempt to mislead the Bologna massacre investigation.
edges.evidence_quote: La probabile convergenza d'interessi tra gli uomini della Magliana, gli ambienti dell'eversione nera e alcuni settori deviati dei servizi e della politica, trova perfetta esemplificazione nel tentativo di depistaggio legato alla strage alla stazione ferroviaria di Bologna del 2 agosto 1980, per la quale vennero riconosciuti esecutori materiali (tra gli altri) alcuni militanti dei Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari

descriptions: Massimo Carminati, an ex-NAR member, was close to Banda della Magliana since the late 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 dicembre 2014 l'ex NAR Massimo Carminati, vicino alla Banda già dalla fine degli anni ‘70, viene arrestato ...

descriptions: Franco Giuseppucci was a contact between the group and NAR.
edges.evidence_quote: tramite del gruppo con gli esponenti dell' eversione nera e dello spontaneismo armato dei Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari

descriptions: Nicolino Selis, a member of the Banda della Magliana, had a close friendship with Raffaele Cutolo, founder of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: vantando numerosi contatti con elementi di spicco della malavita organizzata e una stretta amicizia con il boss campano Raffaele Cutolo , fondatore della Nuova Camorra Organizzata

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana obtained approval from the Nuova Camorra Organizzata to kill Nicolini, and Cutolo appointed Selis as his lieutenant in Rome, establishing cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: La decisione di uccidere Nicolini venne presa dalla banda anche in virtù del beneplacito, ottenuto dal capo della Nuova Camorra Organizzata , Raffaele Cutolo , il quale, appena evaso dall'ospedale psichiatrico di Aversa, nel febbraio del 1978 organizzò un incontro con Nicolino Selis allo scopo di trovare, tra i rispettivi gruppi, una strategia compatibile con gli obiettivi di entrambi, nominando così “il Sardo” suo luogotenente nella piazza romana.

descriptions: Nuova Camorra Organizzata contacted Banda della Magliana regarding the Moro kidnapping.
edges.evidence_quote: Cutolo ci ha mandato un personaggio politico a parlare per vedere se sapevamo dov'era il covo di Moro

descriptions: Nicolino Selis had support from Raffaele Cutolo's Camorra faction.
edges.evidence_quote: forte dell'appoggio dei camorristi di Raffaele Cutolo

descriptions: Claudio Sicilia served as a link between Banda della Magliana and Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: Claudio Sicilia detto il Vesuviano ... diviene ben presto l'anello di congiunzione della banda con la Nuova Camorra Organizzata di Raffaele Cutolo.

descriptions: Nicolino Selis maintained contacts between Banda della Magliana and Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: Nicolino Selis ... si occupa principalmente di tenere i contatti tra l'organizzazione e la Nuova Camorra Organizzata di Raffaele Cutolo.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana fought a war against the Proietti clan.
edges.evidence_quote: guerra al clan rivale dei Proietti

descriptions: The infobox lists Proietti clan as a rival of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Proietti clan

descriptions: The Proietti clan killed Franco Giuseppucci, triggering a violent war between the two groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Il primo componente della Banda a cadere fu Franco Giuseppucci , ucciso a Piazza San Cosimato nel cuore del quartiere di Trastevere, il 13 settembre 1980 , in un agguato orchestrato da parte di esponenti del clan rivale della famiglia Proietti , detti i Pesciaroli

descriptions: The infobox lists Sicilian Mafia as an ally of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NAR, Sicilian Mafia, Camorra

descriptions: Marcello Colafigli, a Banda della Magliana member, maintained contacts with Mafia foggiana for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenendo rapporti con esponenti della 'ndrangheta , della camorra , della mafia foggiana e con albanesi inseriti in un cartello narcos sudamericano

descriptions: Camorra has relationships with the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: La camorra intrattiene rapporti con le associazioni mafiose operanti nella capitale, quali la Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Casamonica clan allied with Banda della Magliana from 1970s to 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The rise to the conquest of the illicit traffic of Rome, occurred, starting from 1970s, with the criminal alliance of the Banda della Magliana, and lasted until the 1990s.

descriptions: Casamonica clan had criminal collusion with Banda della Magliana, which provided contacts.
edges.evidence_quote: Its criminal rise took place in the early 1970s, with criminal collusion with the Banda della Magliana, which provided it with initial contacts with Camorra, 'Ndrangheta and Mafia.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana used Casamonica members as criminal manpower in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni ottanta, per esempio, la Banda della Magliana ha spesso utilizzato componenti del clan come manovalanza criminale

descriptions: The Casamonica clan collaborated with Enrico Nicoletti, former cashier of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Si hanno notizie di collaborazione del clan dei Casamonica con l'ex cassiere della Banda della Magliana, Enrico Nicoletti

descriptions: The two organizations were linked through criminal collaboration in the Tor Vergata construction project.
edges.evidence_quote: la costruzione della seconda università di Roma a Tor Vergata contribuisce a legare il sodalizio criminale tra la Banda della Magliana e il clan Casamonica

descriptions: Members of the Banda della Magliana and Dante Casamonica were involved together in arms and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 20 aprile 1994 scatta una maxi operazione contro il traffico di armi ... e droga che vede coinvolti esponenti della Banda della Magliana , un agente di Polizia e Dante Casamonica .

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana succeeded the Clan dei marsigliesi after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: La successiva unione di alcune di queste batterie diede poi vita a una vera e propria banda per il controllo totale dei traffici illeciti romani conosciuta come Banda della Magliana .

descriptions: The Lubrano-Ligato clan committed murders on orders from the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Il pentito Abate, collaboratore di giustizia, ed il capoclan Ligato, a cavallo tra gli anni '70 e 90, commisero 477 omicidi, per ordine della Banda della Magliana , di Cosa nostra e del clan Nuvoletta .

descriptions: Mafia Capitale is a successor or continuation of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Mafia Capitale (which was partially a successor or continuation of Banda della Magliana, involving many former Banda della Magliana members and associates)

descriptions: Mafia Capitale was founded from the remains of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: It was founded in the early 2000s by Massimo Carminati from the remains of the Banda della Magliana.

descriptions: NCO established strong ties with Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: The NCO also established strong ties with the Apulian Sacra Corona Unita and the Roman Banda della Magliana

descriptions: NCO had a representative in the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Cutolo ... indicò Nicolino Selis - uno dei capi della Banda della Magliana ... quale referente della NCO nella capitale

descriptions: Cutolo made agreements with Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Cutolo stringe anche accordi con le bande lombarde di Renato Vallanzasca e Francis Turatello e quelle pugliesi e la Banda della Magliana romana.

descriptions: Nicolino Selis was Cutolo's representative in the Banda della Magliana, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Nicolino Selis ... referente di Cutolo nella Banda della Magliana a Roma.

White Fence.description: White Fence is a predominantly Mexican American street gang based in Boyle Heights, East Los Angeles, claiming origins as early as 1900 but emerging in the 1910s as a sports team associated with La Purissima Church. It is one of the oldest gangs in Los Angeles, known for early use of firearms and a long-running rivalry with El Hoyo Maravilla. The gang has a structured barrio-based culture and is allied with the Mexican Mafia and Sureños.

descriptions: White Fence member killed Armenian Power founder.
edges.evidence_quote: Armen 'Silent' Petrosyan, a founder of Armenian Power, was shot to death on May 22, 2000 by Jose Argueta, a member of the Sureño White Fence gang.

descriptions: White Fence is listed as a rival of Norteños in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Fresno Bulldogs, Sureños, MS-13, Mexican Mafia, Aryan Brotherhood, 18th Street gang, White Fence, Border Brothers, Tiny Rascal Gang, Oriental Troopz, Mongols MC

descriptions: Temple Street gang is allied with White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sureños , Satanas , Mexican Mafia , Florencia 13 , MS-13 , White Fence

descriptions: Varrio Nuevo Estrada has a rivalry with White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: White Fence

descriptions: Armenian Power is a rival of White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: All Maravilla sets, Varrio Nuevo Estrada , Armenian Power , others flats gangs

descriptions: The Avenues gang originally derived from Varrio White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: gangs such as the Avenues (AVES), Frogtown (FTR), Hazard (BH), and other sections in the area originally derived from Varrio White Fence.

descriptions: White Fence and El Hoyo Maravilla have a long-standing rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivalry between the gang and another Hispanic gang, El Hoyo Maravilla, is one of the longest, ongoing feuds in all of Los Angeles, a rivalry going back to the 1930s.

descriptions: Frogtown gang originally derived from Varrio White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: gangs such as the Avenues (AVES), Frogtown (FTR), Hazard (BH), and other sections in the area originally derived from Varrio White Fence.

descriptions: Hazard gang originally derived from Varrio White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: gangs such as the Avenues (AVES), Frogtown (FTR), Hazard (BH), and other sections in the area originally derived from Varrio White Fence.

descriptions: White Fence has territorial conflicts with KAMSTERS X3.
edges.evidence_quote: White Fence has pushed itself into many territory-based beefs ... engaging in territorial beefs with neighborhoods like Tiny Boys X3 (TBS), KAMSTERS X3 (KAM), Armernian Power X3 (APX3), and many other gangs

descriptions: Mexican Mafia is an ally of White Fence.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños (some other sets)

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: All Maravilla sets, Varrio Nuevo Estrada , Armenian Power , others flats gangs

descriptions: Black Sheep were a Hells Angels prospect club that carried out a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Anker Walther "Høvding" Marcus (January 17, 1947 – December 21, 1985) was then murdered by Ole Bonnesen Nielsen and Rene Nøddeskov Ludvigsen, two members of the Black Sheep (another Hells Angels prospect club), following a Christmas party at Nemoland Café in Christiania on December 21, 1985.

descriptions: Rosmini allied with the Condello-Imerti-Serraino group during the second 'ndrangheta war.
edges.evidence_quote: Nella seconda guerra di 'ndrangheta si schierarono con il gruppo Condello - Imerti - Serraino.

descriptions: Byrd Gang is allied with AHM according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 39ers , BTY, AHM, Gerttown Hounds. Fischer Projects, 3NG

descriptions: KNLA is the armed wing of the KNU.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2006, the Tatmadaw launched a large-scale military offensive against the KNU's armed wing, the Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA).

descriptions: KNLA is the armed wing of KNU.
edges.evidence_quote: the Karen National Union (KNU) and its armed wing the Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA)

descriptions: C Block claims affiliation with Gooch Close Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: also claims affiliation to Gooch and Crips through family ties and friendships

descriptions: Infoboox lists M21 as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: M21

descriptions: Pillow Gang and Russo Gang battled the Green Ones.
edges.evidence_quote: The two gangs battled with the Green Ones until 1928, when the Russo gang lost too many members and blamed the Pillow gang for betraying them.

descriptions: Pillow Gang allied with Russo Gang to battle the Green Ones.
edges.evidence_quote: Fresina formed an alliance with splinter group of the Green Ones called the Russo gang led by Tony Russo.

descriptions: Russo Gang fought with Pillow Gang after the alliance broke.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1931, Fresina was murdered and members of the Pillow gang continued fighting with the Russo gang and the Green Ones.

descriptions: Mumtaz Doghmush, a clan leader, led the Army of Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: Mumtaz Doghmush, who was involved in the kidnapping of Gilad Shalit, led the Army of Islam by 2008.

descriptions: Clan members proclaimed themselves as the Army of Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: Clan members affiliated with Al-Qaeda were involved in the kidnapping and holding of the British journalist Alan Johnston ... The clan members proclaimed themselves as the Jaysh al-Islām (Army of Islam).

County Road Cutters.description: The County Road Cutters are a hooligan firm associated with Premier League football club Everton F.C. They were one of the largest hooligan firms in England during the 1980s and early 1990s. The firm is known for adopting casual clothing and designer garments in the 1970s and has been involved in high-profile clashes, including the 2005 'Battle of Everton Valley' with Manchester United's Red Army.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Chelsea Headhunters.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C., the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals, as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters, a firm associated with Chelsea F.C., and the Millwall Bushwackers, a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Millwall Bushwackers.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C., the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals, as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters, a firm associated with Chelsea F.C., and the Millwall Bushwackers, a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: The Cutters clashed with the Red Army in 2005, known as 'The Battle of Everton Valley'.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cutters have been involved in a number of high-profile clashes with other firms, most notably in 2005 with the Red Army, a firm associated with Manchester United.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Seaburn Casuals.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C., the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals, as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters, a firm associated with Chelsea F.C., and the Millwall Bushwackers, a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: ETA and Breton Revolutionary Army teamed up to steal explosives.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1999 ETA militants teamed up with the (now self-dissolved) Breton Revolutionary Army to steal explosives from magazines in Brittany.

CBL/BFL.description: American street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2009 in the Towne Gardens Housing Complex. Engages in drug trafficking (heroin, fentanyl, cocaine, crack, marijuana) and weapons racketeering. Involved in turf wars with rival gangs, including the Ronnie Gang. Members have been convicted under RICO statutes.

descriptions: The infobox lists Ronnie Gang as a rival of CBL/BFL.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ronnie Gang

descriptions: Fazzari members are in close contact with the Spada clan in Rome.
edges.evidence_quote: esponenti della Famiglia Fazzari in stretto contatto con le locali organizzazioni criminali, tra cui gli Spada

descriptions: Clan Spada contacted Franco Colò, a former Banda della Magliana member, to expand their videopoker business.
edges.evidence_quote: il gruppo degli "zingari" per allargare i propri traffici attraverso i videopoker si fossero messi in contatto con Franco Colò detto "il ciccione", storico braccio destro di Renatino De Pedis ai tempi della Banda

descriptions: The Casamonica and Spada clans are described as a single mafia organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan dei Casamonica e degli Spada sono un' organizzazione criminale mafiosa

descriptions: The Spada clan is described as contiguous to the Casamonica clan, indicating close association.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli atti di violenza e intimidazione riportano l'attenzione sull'infiltrazione del metodo mafioso nella capitale, già segnalata per altri simili episodi ad opera del clan Spada , contiguo a quello dei Casamonica.

descriptions: Spada family is affiliated with Casamonica clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Police operations that led to the arrest not only of members of the Clan, but also of members of other affiliated families of Sinti origin, such as the Spada and the Di Silvio

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabaab has Kenya-based affiliates, with al-Hijra as its official Kenyan wing from 2012

descriptions: SSMC is part of a coalition with The Creativity Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: Their club's motto, "The Black & Silver Solution"', is the phrase given to represent a coalition of several racist organizations with the United Klans of America and The Creativity Movement.

South Brooklyn Boys.description: The South Brooklyn Boys (SBB) was a New York City street gang formed in the 1950s from several Italian-American neighborhood gangs in South Brooklyn. The gang engaged in assault, drug trafficking, extortion, bookmaking, and murder, and had loose affiliations with the Italian-American Mafia. It was active from the 1950s until the 2000s.

descriptions: The American Mafia lists South Brooklyn Boys as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta , Sacra corona unita , varias pandillas independientes ítaloestadounidenses (como la 10th & Oregon Crew , Purple Gang , South Brooklyn Boys , y Tanglewood Boys )

descriptions: Infobox lists South Brooklyn Boys as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: South Brooklyn Boys

descriptions: Lucchese family lists South Brooklyn Boys as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: South Brooklyn Boys

descriptions: South Brooklyn Boys has been loosely affiliated with and worked for the Italian-American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang has been loosely affiliated with and has worked for the Italian-American Mafia throughout its history to the present, but it is not an official Mafia crew.

descriptions: Butler Gents was a constituent gang that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the gangs that made up the original South Brooklyn Boys were the South Brooklyn Devils, the Garfield Place Boys, the SB Angels, SB Diapers, the Wanderers, the Degraw St boys, the Sackett St Boys, the Butler Gents, the Gowanus Boys, the Kane St. Midgets, The Little Gents, and the Young Savages.

descriptions: The Chaplains were bitter enemies of South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Also bitter enemies of SBB were the Puerto Rican Gangs, The Untouchable Bishops, and the Apaches, and the African American gangs The Mau Maus, and the Chaplains.
